key_insight: ACPO improves token-level credit assignment in RL for LLM reasoning by using a mode-local surrogate entropy that selectively sharpens learning on uncertain correct tokens and overconfident incorrect tokens.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more scalable and gradient-aligned way to train reasoning models than uniform trajectory rewards or non-local true-entropy objectives.
method: Uses adaptive token-level credit instead of uniform trajectory-level assignment.
Applies an asymmetric surrogate entropy: emphasize uncertain decisions in successful rollouts and overconfident tokens in failed rollouts.
Provides deterministic entropy bounds and a proximal-update argument for preserving the policy-gradient direction to leading order.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent gains over strong RL baselines such as DAPO, GTPO, and SAPO on mathematical reasoning and coding benchmarks including AIME 2025 and HumanEvalPro.
watch_item: The surrogate guarantees depend on modal alignment and proximal updates, so performance may be sensitive to how well these assumptions hold in practice.
paper_title: ACPO: Adaptive Credit Policy Optimization via Fine-Grained Surrogate Entropy
